data.title: Royalty Pharma declares $0.22 dividend
data.description:
• Royalty Pharma (NASDAQ:RPRX) declared $0.22/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.48%
  • Payable Sept. 10; for shareholders of record Aug. 15; ex-div Aug. 15.
  • See RPRX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: 3M shares jump to four-year high after Q2 beat and raise
data.description:
3M (NYSE:MMM) +3.1% pre-market Friday, poised to open at its highest since September 2021, after posting better than expected Q2 adjusted earnings and revenues while raising its full-year outlook, as cost-cutting measures and efforts to focus on high-margin products begin to pay off.

Q2 profit fell to $723M, or $1.34/share, from $1.15B, or $2.07/share, in the year-earlier quarter, but adjusted earnings of $2.16/share exceeded $1.93/share reported a year ago and topped Wall Street consensus of $2.01/share, and adjusted operating margin, a key metric for the company, was 24.5%, ahead of estimates of 23.6%.

3M (NYSE:MMM) hiked its FY 2025 guidance for adjusted EPS to $7.75-$8.00 from its prior outlook of $7.60-$7.90, with the new guidance including additional costs from tariffs, and said is the result of improving execution and solid results in this year's H1.

The company also sees full-year total sales growth of ~2.5%, reflecting adjusted organic sales growth of ~2%, and adjusted operating cash flow of $5.1B-$5.5B, contributing to greater than 100% adjusted free cash flow conversion.

Under CEO William Brown, 3M (NYSE:MMM) has taken steps to ease the impact of tariffs, including shifting production and pricing changes, but the company previously said tariffs could have a negative impact of as much as $0.40/share on full-year earnings.

data.title: Western Digital added to Tactical Outperform List at Evercore, price target raised
data.description:
Evercore added Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) to its Tactical Outperform List, while maintaining its Outperform rating on the stock and raising the price target to $77 from $55.

Analysts led by Amit Daryanani said Western Digital's June-quarter should come in-line or better than expectations supported by robust cloud spending and AI-related deployments.

Importantly, nearly 80% of Western Digital's hyperscale sales are under long-term agreements, providing 12 months of demand visibility and supply control, particularly useful amid ongoing tariff uncertainties, the analysts added.

Preliminary IDC data indicates that Hard Disk Drive, or HDD, unit shipments came in line with forecast (29.6M units, down -2.4% year-over-year), while revenues are expected to be up over 26% year-over-year — implying average selling price, or ASP, is up about 29% year-over-year from higher capacity mix and strong pricing environment.

The analysts noted that particularly, Nearline EB shipments are projected to reach around 330 exabytes (up 38% year-over-year), with the company capturing an estimated 48% to 50% market share.

Encouragingly, the analysts added, that they see the potential for margins to exceed 40%, with operating margins expanding beyond the company's 24% target, supported by the ramp of three hyperscalers for UltraSMR, a margin-accretive product due to its software-enabled features.

While Western Digital's Heat-Assisted Magnetic Recording, or HAMR, technology roadmap and qualification timeline remain key investor focus areas, the analysts noted that, the company was relatively conservative with its long-term targets at its recent Investor Day, baking in 6 to 7 points of potential pricing headwinds, and they see upside if the current pricing environment holds.

Daryanani and his team said that Western Digital is progressing on deleveraging efforts, aided by the Sandisk business spinoff, and should see lower interest expense in the June-quarter (completed Sandisk debt-for-equity exchange) and appears close to launching its $2B share repurchase program.

Shares of Western Digital rose about 1% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Safe Pro selected by U.S. government contractor to provide protection gear in Indo-Asia Pacific region
data.description:
• Safe Pro Group (NASDAQ:SPAI) said on Friday that it has received a new order from a U.S. government contractor operating supporting the US Department of State landmine and explosive ordnance remediation initiatives in the Indo-Asia Pacific region. 
  • The order includes the supply of U.S.-manufactured ballistic and explosive ordnance disposal protective equipment, which is scheduled for delivery in the third quarter of 2025.
  • In addition to planned EOD and ballistic protective gear deliveries, Safe Pro will demonstrate in theater its proprietary drone-powered artificial intelligence platform, SpotlightAI.

data.title: Burberry rallies in London trading after earnings show signs of a turnaround
data.description:
Burberry (OTCPK:BURBY) reported revenue fell by 2% to £433 million for the three-month period that ended on June 28. Same-store sales were down 1% for the period to mark a notable improvement from the prior quarter. Sales were higher in Europe and the Americas, but the Asia business slumped again.

The London-based company pointed to a tough macro environment, but expressed optimism about the quarters ahead.

CEO Joshua Schulman said the improvement in first quarter comparable sales, strength in core categories, and uptick in brand desirability gives the retailer conviction in the path ahead. "Our Autumn 2025 collection is being well received by a broad range of luxury customers as it arrives in stores. Although the external environment remains challenging and we are still in the early stages of our transformation, we are encouraged by the initial progress we are starting to see," he added.

Morgan Stanley analyst Grace Smalley said the Burberry (OTCPK:BURBY) print showed further initial successful strides on the company's strategic turnaround and confirmed recent positive channel checks. "We expect investor debate now to turn to the sustainability of the continued sequential improvement in LFL trends as Burberry begins to face a tougher comparison base in H2 (at least on a yoy basis) and lap some of management's initial turnaround efforts – while in the near-term facing sector-wide tourism headwinds in Q2 (=seasonally over-indexes to tourism)," highlighted Smalley.

Burberry (OTCPK:BURBY) shot up 7.0% in London trading on Friday.

data.title: Optimi Health launches $3.45M convertible debenture offering
data.description:
• Optimi Health (OTCQX:OPTHF) announces a non-brokered private placement of 3,450 unsecured convertible debentures at CAD$1,000 each.
  • Total gross proceeds from the offering will be CAD$3.45M.
  • Debentures bear 15% annual interest, payable quarterly, and mature in 12–24 months, depending on investor election.
  • Each debenture is convertible into common shares at CAD$0.15.
  • Proceeds will be used for general working capital.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about July 24, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: FOXO Technologies to spin off its epigenetics subsidiary
data.description:
• FOXO Technologies (NYSE:FOXO) said on Thursday that its board has approved pursuing the spin-off of its FOXO Labs subsidiary that is focused on the development of its epigenetics business.
  • The Epigenetics subsidiary has been serving as a pioneer in the development and integration of epigenetic biomarkers into underwriting protocols and consumer engagement tools. 
  • “We believe spinning this business off creates the best opportunity to deliver our business strategy and create value for FOXO and its shareholders,” said Mark White, Chief Executive Officer of FOXO Labs. 
  • “We believe a spin-off, whereby FOXO shareholders benefit from directly owning FOXO Labs and by FOXO Technologies retaining some ownership in the spun-off company, is a strategy that facilitates a simplified business model for each entity. We look forward to delivering this spinoff as soon as practical,” said Seamus Lagan, Chief Executive Officer of FOXO Technologies. 
  • FOXO Technologies (NYSE:FOXO) shares were down more than 9% in premarket trading.

data.title: TD Asset Management announces merger, discontinuation of funds
data.description:
• TD Asset Management ("TDAM") said on Friday that its discontinuing funds will be merged into continuing funds, effective October 24, 2025.
  • TD FundSmart Managed Aggressive Growth Portfolio will merge with TD Managed Aggressive Growth Portfolio, TD FundSmart Managed Balanced Growth Portfolio will merge with TD Managed Balanced Growth Portfolio, and TD FundSmart Managed Income & Moderate Growth Portfolio will merge with TD Managed Income & Moderate Growth Portfolio.
  • The company has also announced the termination of certain TD Mutual Funds including TD Global Conservative Opportunities Fund, TD Global Balanced Opportunities Fund.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Viatris down on late-stage trial setback for eye disorder therapy
data.description:
Viatris (NASDAQ:VTRS) shares fell in the premarket on Friday after the company announced that its experimental therapy for blepharitis, an inflammatory condition of the eyelids, did not reach the main goal in a late-stage trial.

Citing data from its MR-139 3001 Phase 3 Study, the Pfizer (PFE) spinoff said that the ophthalmic ointment named pimecrolimus 0.3% (MR-139) did not reach the trial’s primary endpoint related to complete resolution of debris over six weeks.

The 477-patient placebo-controlled trial evaluated MR-139, self-administered to the eyelids, with a twice-daily dosing regimen over 12 weeks.

"Given that the study did not meet its objective for patients suffering from blepharitis, we are evaluating the appropriate next steps for the Phase 3 program,” Viatris’ (NASDAQ:VTRS) R&D chief Philippe Martin remarked.

Changing the planned additional Phase 3 study for the drug is also among those plans, he added.

data.title: Kraig Biocraft nears three-year government collaboration to expand spider silk production
data.description:
• Kraig Biocraft Laboratories (OTCQB:KBLB) said on Friday that it is finalizing a three-year collaborative agreement with a key government agency in Southeast Asia to expand the scale and security of its recombinant spider silk manufacturing platform.
  • Under the terms of this collaboration, Kraig Labs will work closely with government specialists to optimize and expand spider silk production. 
  • The collaboration will unlock new production capacity, enabling Kraig Labs to significantly scale its hybridized silkworm operations.
  • Importantly, this agreement also includes provisions for the creation of parallel rearing operations, a key component in the company’s long-term roadmap to mitigate production risk and eliminate single points of failure. 
  • Work under this agreement is anticipated to begin in August.
